row1.revenue: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with $716.9B in revenue, narrowly edging out Walmart Inc. (WMT) by $3.7B. The top two companies hold a significant revenue lead over the next closest competitor, with a $169.4B gap to UnitedHealth Group Incorporated (UNH).
row1.net_income: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with a Net Income of $132.2B, a significant gap of over $30B from NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) in second place. This substantial difference highlights GOOGL's dominant financial performance in the latest fiscal year.
row1.total_assets: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with $4.4T in Total Assets, a significant gap from Bank of America Corporation (BAC) at $3.4T. This 29% difference highlights JPM's substantial market dominance.
row1.total_equity: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) holds the top spot with $415.3B in Total Equity, narrowly surpassing Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) by $4.2B. The leader maintains a significant gap of over $50B compared to the third-ranked JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM).
row1.total_liabilities: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with Total Liabilities of 4.1T USD, a significant gap from second-place Bank of America Corporation (BAC) at 3.1T USD. This 28% gap highlights the substantial difference in financial leverage between the two banking giants.
row1.cash_and_equivalents: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with $343.3B in Cash And Equivalents, a significant gap ahead of Bank of America Corporation (BAC) at $239.3B. This substantial difference underscores JPM's strong liquidity position relative to its peers.
row1.gross_profit: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with a Gross Profit of 360.5B USD, more than 1.5 times that of Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) at 240.3B USD, indicating a significant gap between the top two companies.
row1.operating_income_ebit: Apple Inc. (AAPL) leads the pack with an impressive Operating Income Ebit of 133.1B USD, a significant margin above NVIDIA Corporation's (NVDA) 130.4B USD. The top two companies hold a substantial 2.7B USD lead over Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) in third place.

row2_sector.revenue: Consumer Cyclical and Technology sectors dominate the revenue landscape, accounting for 43.1% of the total with a combined $1.913B USD. This concentration of revenue in these two sectors may pose a risk to diversified portfolios.
row2_sector.total_assets: Financial Services sector dominates the total assets landscape with 64.1% share, nearly 7 times larger than the next closest sector, Technology. This concentration highlights the sector's significant influence on the overall financial health of the listed companies.
row2_sector.net_income: Technology sector dominates net income with a 39.2% share, significantly outpacing the next largest sector, Communication Services, at 24.8%. This concentration of net income in Technology suggests a high degree of sectoral influence on overall corporate profitability.

row2_bubble: NVDA's 55.6% net margin significantly outpaces UNH's 2.7%, highlighting a stark contrast in profitability efficiency between the two companies. This disparity may be attributed to NVDA's strong brand presence and UNH's high operating expenses.
row3_heatmap: Morgan Stanley (MA) stands out for its exceptional Return on Equity (ROE) of 193.5%, while JPMorgan Chase (JPM) takes the opposite end with a high Debt/Equity ratio of 11.21x, indicating significant leverage risk.
row4_market_trends: GOOGL leads the pack with a 12-month cumulative return of +110.1%, outpacing the next best performer by 63.5%. HD takes the bottom spot with a -6.7% return, the only company in the group to post a positive gain.
